Protected Content (Widevine/EME) Test Fixture

Exercises the Encrypted Media Extensions path that DRM web players (e.g. Spotify) use. On Android, the first Widevine probe triggers WebSpace's "Play protected content?" popup (the PROTECTED_MEDIA_ID permission). Your Allow/Block choice is remembered per-site.

EME API availability

navigator.requestMediaKeySystemAccess: checking...

If unavailable, the platform has no EME support (expected on iOS/macOS WKWebView) and nothing below can succeed.

Widevine (triggers the DRM popup)

Result: not run

First run on Android: expect the Allow/Block popup. After Allow, the promise resolves and a CDM is created. Reload the page — there should be NO second popup (decision remembered). Set the site's "Protected content (DRM)" dropdown to Block and reload — the probe should fail with no popup.

ClearKey (control — should NOT trigger the popup)

Result: not run

ClearKey needs no CDM or device identifier, so it should resolve without any popup. If a popup appears here, the permission handler is granting more than protected media.

Event Log
